coverage_notes:
ML 'Big Three' oral papers, 2012-2020, filling the gap before the existing orals_openreview.json (which covers 2021-2026). Oral = a SELECTIVE honor (the minority of accepted papers chosen to give a talk); years where every accepted paper gave an equal-length talk are skipped, not collected as 'all oral'. Workshop/track papers are excluded. No entries fabricated.

=== ICLR 2013-2020 (164 entries) ===
2013: 14. OpenReview api1 submission notes with decision=='conferenceOral-iclr2013-conference'; the 7 workshop-track orals (conferenceOral-iclr2013-workshop) and all posters/rejects excluded. 2014: 14. Official ICLR 2014 conference program (Banff), archived Google Sites page via Wayback, scheduled 18 paper oral talks; 4 of them were workshop-track submissions (confirmed present in OpenReview ICLR.cc/2014/workshop/-/submission) and are excluded to keep conference-track orals only, matching the 2013 rule. OpenReview has 2014 conference submissions but recorded no oral/poster decision ('submitted, no decision'), so the program archive was the source; source_url=arXiv. 2015: 11 'Conference Oral Presentations' from the iclr.cc archived program (ICLR 2015 not on OpenReview); source_url=arXiv (Adam was a poster that year, correctly not listed as oral). 2016: 15 oral talks (5/day x 3 days) from the iclr.cc archived program, drawn from ~80 conference-track accepted papers; source_url=arXiv; includes the 2 best papers (Neural Programmer-Interpreters, Deep Compression). 2017: 15. OpenReview api1 submission content.venue=='ICLR 2017 Oral'. 2018: 23. OpenReview Acceptance_Decision content.decision=='Accept (Oral)'. 2019: 24. OpenReview Meta_Review content.recommendation=='Accept (Oral)'. 2020: 48. OpenReview Decision content.decision=='Accept (Talk)'; the 108 'Accept (Spotlight)' and all posters excluded. ICLR counts 2017/2018/2019/2020 (15/23/24/48) match the widely-cited figures. source_url is the OpenReview forum for 2013 and 2017-2020, arXiv for 2014-2016 (arXiv ids spot-verified to resolve to the claimed titles).

=== NeurIPS/NIPS 2012-2020 (331 entries) ===
Method: nips.cc/Conferences/<year>/Schedule?type=Oral (live endpoint) parsed for oral-session cards, then intersected with the official proceedings title list (papers.nips.cc) so only real accepted papers are kept; this drops non-paper oral events (invited talks, Test-of-Time award talks). authors and source_url taken from the proceedings paper page. Only orals collected -- spotlights are a separate schedule type and excluded. Counts: 2012=20, 2013=20, 2014=20, 2015=15, 2016=45, 2017=40, 2018=30, 2019=36, 2020=105. 2020 independently cross-checked against the neurips.cc virtual JSON (neurips-2020-orals-posters.json): 106 Oral eventtype events, of which one is the Test-of-Time talk on a 2011 paper (Hogwild!), leaving 105 real 2020 oral papers -- exact agreement. 3 NeurIPS 2012 orals whose schedule talk-title differed from the proceedings title were resolved by hand to their proceedings entry (Transelliptical Component Analysis; Structure estimation for discrete graphical models...; High-Order Multi-Task Feature Learning...Alzheimer's...).

=== ICML 2012-2020 (508 entries; only selective-oral years collected) ===
2013: 137. ICML 2013 detailed schedule (icml.cc/2013 archived) had a clean two-tier structure -- full oral talks vs 'Spotlight Presentations' -- with 137 orals + 144 spotlights (approx 283 total = PMLR v28). Oral titles cross-referenced to PMLR v28 for canonical title/authors/URL (12 schedule-vs-proceedings title-wording differences resolved by hand). 2018: 213. From icml.cc schedule: every accepted paper got a talk, split into 20-min 'long talks' (213, the selective top tier, collected as oral) and 10-min short talks (408, spotlight-equivalent, excluded). Cross-referenced to PMLR v80. 2019: 158. Same structure: 20-min long talks (158, collected) vs 5-min short talks (615, excluded). Cross-referenced to PMLR v97. Matches the known ICML 2019 20-min long-talk tier. SKIPPED years (no selective oral tier): 2012 -- schedule shows a 20-min/5-min timing split but ~65% of papers got the 20-min slot, there is no official oral/poster label, the split is only inferable from schedule timing, and ICML 2012 predates PMLR so titles/authors cannot be verified against a proceedings volume; too weak/unverifiable to treat as a selective oral honor. 2014 -- every accepted paper received an equal 20-min talk (schedule: '6x20 minute talks, 6 sessions in parallel'), no selective tier. 2015 -- official booklet states 'Each paper is presented orally during a session and in the poster session'; everyone oral. 2016 -- every paper an equal ~17-min talk plus poster. 2017 -- every paper an equal 18-min talk plus poster; the modern icml.cc schedule has 0 events of type 'Oral'. 2020 -- fully virtual; official icml.cc virtual JSON (icml-2020-orals-posters.json) classifies all 1084 papers as Poster with no Oral eventtype and no oral sessions. So ICML has a selective oral/long-talk tier only in 2013, 2018, 2019 (of 2012-2020); the 2014-2017 all-equal-talk era and the 2020 virtual all-poster year are excluded, and 2012 is excluded as unverifiable/weak.

year_work == year_awarded for every entry (no retrospective awards in this set). Every entry traces to an OpenReview note id, a papers.nips.cc proceedings page, a PMLR proceedings URL, or an arXiv id from an official archived conference program.
